FT_IC1.evidence: Uses U.S. ABCD Study baseline data from 11,834 children ages 9-10

FT_IC5.evidence: Reports original empirical confirmatory factor analysis and regression analysis on ABCD data

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed conference paper (2021 IEEE International Conference on Bioinformatics and Biomedicine)

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1: No phone/SM-specific variable with coefficient found. Step 2: Composite p factor outcome (psychopathology latent variable) analyzed with screen time as contextual predictor alongside sleep, physical activity, household income, gender. Screen time appears only in mixed-effects model explaining variance in p factor; no per-modality phone/SM coefficient reported. Other side (p factor) is transdiagnostic composite, not individual-trait-level. No qualifying SM/phone-individual-trait pairing.

notes: Exclusion code: FT-EC2 (no qualifying smartphone/SM variable with individual-trait-level other side)

FT_IC1.evidence: Discusses the U.S. ABCD Study, baseline ages 9-10, 21 U.S. recruitment sites

FT_IC5.evidence: Commentary/editorial paper labeled as such in journal header. Describes ABCD study architecture, data opportunities, and responsible data use. Does not report original empirical analyses.

FT_IC1.evidence: Baseline cross-sectional data from U.S. ABCD Study; 10,712 children ages 9-10 from 21 research sites

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical latent profile analysis and multilevel logistic regression on ABCD baseline data

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed journal article (2021, International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health)

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1: No phone/SM-specific variable with coefficient found in results. Step 2: Screen time is bundled as one of three components of a composite outcome (meeting 24-h movement behavior recommendations: physical activity ≥60 min/day + screen time ≤2 h/day + sleep 9-11 h/night) with no separate phone/SM breakout. Odds ratios reported for meeting ≥1, ≥2, or all 3 recommendations, but screen time not isolated. Other side (family typologies of parental acceptance, monitoring, conflict) are contextual/family environment variables, not individual-trait-level per §2.4. Contextual-only pairing fails FT-IC2.

notes: Exclusion code: FT-EC2 (screen time bundled in composite without isolation; other side is contextual/family functioning, not individual-trait-level)
